body:
## Summary
- Add `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` parsing and `resolve-task-timeout` CLI support.
- Resolve timeout minutes before every `run-agent-task` workflow/template step and wire `timeout-minutes` from the resolver output.
- Document the policy and add parser, CLI, docs, and workflow contract tests.

## Verification
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/task-timeout-policy.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/resolve-task-timeout-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #99

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777762688311 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjM4Mjc0MTU6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojk5OnJldmlldzoxMDA6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR closes #99, which is the child of parent #72; the diff implements the narrowed GitHub-timeout-only plan approved there. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` with explicit `default_minutes` and `route_overrides`, and resolves the current `ROUTE` separately instead of overloading a string. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Adds typed `.agent/src/task-timeout-policy.ts` and CLI `.agent/src/cli/resolve-task-timeout.ts`, reusing the existing `setOutput` helper and matching memory/rubrics policy parser style. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md` and adds docs coverage in `.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Malformed timeout policy returns code `2` with `Invalid AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` instead of silently falling back to a misleading clean timeout. |
| coding_style / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are limited to the new timeout policy, resolver, direct workflow call sites for `run-agent-task`, docs, and focused tests. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Keeps the first pass as an outer GitHub Actions `timeout-minutes` guard and does not thread timeout into `run.ts`, `runAcpx`, or `acpx-adapter`. |

## Notes

- The strongest score drivers are the typed policy/CLI implementation, clear invalid-policy failure behavior, and a workflow contract test requiring every `run-agent-task` step to be guarded by the resolver.
- I verified the locally available compiled envelope suite with `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`; the new source tests are present in the PR diff, but the local `dist` tree in this checkout did not include those newly compiled files.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #100 adds configurable GitHub Actions step timeouts for Sepo agent task runs.
- It closes #99, a child of #72, and implements the approved first pass: an outer GitHub Actions timeout only.
- The change adds `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY`, a typed parser, a resolver CLI, workflow wiring for every `run-agent-task` use, docs, and focused tests.
- Inner `acpx` timeout handling and typed timeout responses are intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Review
The implementation matches the requested scope and the rubric review passed, but two small validation/test gaps remain. There was no meaningful reviewer disagreement: both code reviews identified the max-timeout validation issue, and one also requested direct CLI success-path coverage.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Timeout policy allows unsupported durations | WARNING | [`normalizeMinutes`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3c619d468bea47f5ff961a57c951e4425570aadc/.agent/src/task-timeout-policy.ts#L24) accepts any positive integer, but GitHub Actions step `timeout-minutes` has a maximum of 360. Values like `1000` can pass parser validation and fail later at workflow runtime. |
| CLI success path is not directly tested | WARNING | [`runResolveTaskTimeoutCli`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/3c619d468bea47f5ff961a57c951e4425570aadc/.agent/src/cli/resolve-task-timeout.ts#L22) has malformed-policy coverage, but no test asserts return code `0` and the `minutes` output for valid input. |
| Optional docs polish remains | INFO | One reviewer noted that internal route names such as `dispatch` and `skill` are only discoverable in workflows, and the workflow fallback `|| '30'` duplicates the TypeScript default. These are non-blocking polish notes. |

## Progress
- The PR implements the scoped GitHub-timeout-only design approved in #72 and delegated through #99.
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific issues.
- Docs and workflow contract tests are already present.
- Existing inline review comments were empty before synthesis; one inline comment was posted on `.agent/src/task-timeout-policy.ts:25` for the max-bound issue.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Timeout policy allows unsupported durations</summary>

**Cause:** The parser validates only positive integers, then workflows pass the resolver output directly to `timeout-minutes`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a `MAX_TASK_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 360` guard in `normalizeMinutes`, reject larger values with a clear error, and add parser/docs coverage for the bound.

**Comments:** GitHub documents step `timeout-minutes` as a positive integer with a [maximum of 360 minutes](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-syntax#jobsjob_idstepsstepstimeout-minutes).
</details>

<details>
<summary>CLI success path is not directly tested</summary>

**Cause:** The CLI test covers route override resolution and malformed-policy failure, but not the side-effecting success contract.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a test that calls `runResolveTaskTimeoutCli` with valid env, verifies exit code `0`, and captures `GITHUB_OUTPUT` or the output helper behavior to assert `minutes` is written.

**Comments:** This is small coverage, but it protects the workflow-facing contract.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Optional docs polish remains</summary>

**Cause:** The new docs give an example policy but do not list all route values used by workflows, and workflow expressions keep a literal fallback default.

**Candidate solutions:** Document route names only if maintainers want that operator-facing detail; leave the fallback as-is unless default drift becomes a practical concern.

**Comments:** Not required for this PR’s current scope.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR

The unresolved warnings are concrete parser/docs/test updates and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Enforce a 360-minute maximum for `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` values and add parser/docs coverage for that bound.
- [ ] Add a `runResolveTaskTimeoutCli` success-path test that verifies exit code `0` and the `minutes` output.

- Enforced the 1-360 minute GitHub Actions step-timeout bound in [`task-timeout-policy.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-99/codex-25263827415/.agent/src/task-timeout-policy.ts).
- Added parser max-bound coverage, docs-contract coverage, and direct resolver CLI success-path output coverage.
- Verification: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/task-timeout-policy.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/resolve-task-timeout-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`; `git diff --check`.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR #100 closes #99, whose body explicitly scopes the child task from parent #72 to a GitHub-timeout-only first pass. The diff follows that narrowed plan. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` as JSON with separate `default_minutes` and `route_overrides`, and each resolver step passes an explicit `ROUTE`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Implements policy parsing in `.agent/src/task-timeout-policy.ts` and a CLI in `.agent/src/cli/resolve-task-timeout.ts`, reusing the existing `setOutput` helper instead of embedding parsing in workflow shell. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | not applicable | N/A | PR does not add preview, dry-run, or read-only behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/customization/configuration-list.md` with the new policy, default, example, and 1-360 minute bound; `envelope.test.ts` asserts docs coverage. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Surface stateful failures | pass | 7/7 | Invalid timeout policy returns exit code `2` and logs `Invalid AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY`; tests cover malformed policy failure rather than silent fallback. |
| coding_style / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to timeout policy parsing, resolver CLI, workflow/template timeout wiring, focused docs, and tests. No unrelated refactors are present. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Matches the approved first pass by applying GitHub Actions `timeout-minutes` only, without threading timeout into `run.ts`, `runAcpx`, or `acpx-adapter`. |

## Notes

- The current head includes fixes from the prior review cycle: `MAX_TASK_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 360`, parser coverage for values over 360, docs coverage for the bound, and direct CLI success-path output coverage.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #100 adds `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY` for configurable GitHub Actions step timeouts on Sepo agent tasks.
- It closes #99, which is the task-timeout-policy child of parent issue #72.
- The approved scope is a GitHub-timeout-only first pass: no `run.ts`, `runAcpx`, or `acpx-adapter` timeout plumbing.
- Current head `7838f4d6499620acb7b4424be3562dfe01af5b43` includes the follow-up fixes from the prior review round.

## Review
The current PR head is ready to ship. Both prior WARNING findings were fixed, and the remaining reviewer comments are INFO-level operator-docs polish rather than required branch work.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues | INFO | The 1-360 minute bound, docs coverage for that bound, and CLI success-path output test are now present. Remaining notes are optional route-name/default-fallback polish only. |

## Progress
- Prior review requested enforcing GitHub’s 360-minute step timeout bound; current head adds `MAX_TASK_TIMEOUT_MINUTES = 360`.
- Prior review requested CLI success-path coverage; current head adds output-contract coverage for `runResolveTaskTimeoutCli`.
- Rubrics review passes with no required follow-up.
- Codex review reports build plus targeted parser, CLI, and envelope tests passing with 68 tests total.
- Existing inline max-bound feedback is already covered by the fix; no new inline comments were posted.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION

No unresolved actionable branch changes remain, so automation should stop and leave any optional docs polish to maintainer preference.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

Correct: PR #100 does **not** add one global wall-clock timeout for the entire orchestrated chain.

What it adds is a per-`run-agent-task` GitHub Actions step timeout. For the orchestrator workflow specifically, it resolves `ROUTE: orchestrator` and applies `timeout-minutes` only to the `Plan next action with agent` step: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7838f4d6499620acb7b4424be3562dfe01af5b43/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml#L133-L146

So the behavior is:

- The orchestrator planner agent run is bounded, defaulting to `30` minutes unless `AGENT_TASK_TIMEOUT_POLICY.route_overrides.orchestrator` overrides it.
- Each downstream route run, like implement/review/fix-pr, gets its own route-specific step timeout.
- There is still no single “whole process must finish within N minutes” timer spanning the full orchestration chain.
- The chain is bounded structurally by rounds, e.g. `automation_current_round < automation_max_rounds`, default max `5`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/7838f4d6499620acb7b4424be3562dfe01af5b43/.agent/src/cli/orchestrator-preflight.ts#L33-L36

Also, this is still the GitHub-timeout-only first pass, so a timeout kills the step externally rather than producing a typed `timed_out` agent response.

One quick question: right now for the orchestrate route: it doesn't limit the time for the whole process right?

@sepo-agent /answer

Correct. In the current `main` checkout, the orchestrator route does **not** have a time limit for the whole process.

Specifically:

- `.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml` runs the planner through `./.github/actions/run-agent-task`, but that step has no `timeout-minutes`.
- There is also no global wall-clock timeout across the full orchestrated chain.
- The only built-in bound is structural: `automation_current_round < automation_max_rounds`, defaulting to max `5` rounds in `.agent/src/cli/orchestrator-preflight.ts`.

So an orchestrated flow is round-limited, not time-limited. A GitHub Actions job may still hit GitHub’s platform/job limits, but Sepo currently does not enforce its own whole-process timeout.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #100.

no rubric changes

I read PR #100 metadata, issue comments, review comments, reviews, diff, linked issue #99, parent issue #72, collaborator/owner signals, and existing active rubrics. The PR conversation did not contain a trusted new durable user/team preference that is not already covered.

The reusable lessons present are already represented by existing rubrics like `simple-implementation`, `prefer-explicit-structured-inputs`, `keep-docs-in-sync`, `read-docs-first`, and `abstract-learned-rubrics`. The remaining details are project-specific timeout phasing or bot/agent advisory review content, so I left the rubrics branch unchanged.

title: Add configurable agent task timeouts
